918 BF16 TFLOPS (v6e) vs 197 (v5e) ≈ 4.66×; matches Google's stated 4.7×. (MXU array dimensions are not published in the spec tables, so the corpus attributes the gain to larger MXUs + higher clock without asserting exact dimensions.)

citations.value: ~5× per chip (2,307 vs 459 BF16 TFLOPS); ~10× pod/system-level
citations.note: Per-chip ratio re-derived (2,307/459 = 5.03×); Google's headline ~10× is a pod/system-level figure
Google Cloud Ironwood announcement blog; also derivable from 2307/918 ≈ 2.5×, so 4× likely includes memory/ICI improvements
Derived: 2,307 TFLOPS ÷ 7,380 GB/s ≈ 313 FLOPs/byte. Not stated explicitly in docs; computed from official chip specs.
